name: MoneyGram-CLOSED
description_short: MoneyGram is a money transfer and payment services company enabling consumers to send and receive money nationwide through online and in-person channels at thousands of agent locations.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company that operates a nationwide network of agent locations where consumers can conduct various financial transactions. The company has built its business model around providing convenient access to money movement services through both digital and physical channels, allowing customers to choose their preferred method of transaction.

MoneyGram's core service offering includes domestic money transfers, allowing customers to send and receive money across the United States. The company operates through a location-based network where customers can walk into agent locations to conduct transactions in person, while also providing online options for those who prefer digital channels. Their services are designed to be accessible to consumers who need quick, reliable money movement solutions.

What distinguishes MoneyGram is its extensive physical footprint combined with online capabilities, giving consumers flexibility in how they access services. The company emphasizes convenience by allowing customers to search for nearby locations via map, address, city, state, or ZIP code. This dual-channel approach—online and in-person—positions them as a hybrid solution for consumers who may prefer either method depending on their situation or comfort level with technology.

MoneyGram serves consumers who need reliable money transfer options but may not have traditional banking relationships or prefer the security of in-person transactions. The company's strength lies in its network accessibility and established brand recognition. However, consumers should compare their fees and exchange rates against other providers, as money transfer services typically charge for convenience and speed. The website provided is primarily a location-finder tool, limiting detailed visibility into their complete fee structure or competitive positioning on this page.
pros: Nationwide network of physical agent locations for in-person transactions
Online and in-person transaction options for consumer flexibility
Easy location search functionality using map, address, city, state, or ZIP
Established brand with decades of money transfer experience
No requirement for bank account to send or receive money
Same-day money availability at pickup locations
cons: Money transfer services typically charge higher fees than traditional bank transfers
Exchange rates on international transfers may not be competitive versus banking alternatives
Limited transparency on fee structure visible from the location-finder website
Dependent on agent location availability rather than true 24/7 access
Requires travel to physical location for in-person transactions despite online option
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts
People sending money to family or friends quickly across the U.S.
Consumers who prefer in-person transactions for security and verification
Individuals needing money transfer services in areas with limited banking infrastructure
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers who need reliable domestic money transfer services and prefer either in-person transactions or online options without a traditional bank account. The main caveat is that money transfer services charge fees for convenience and speed, so consumers should compare their rates against traditional banking alternatives before using the service for regular transfers.
services: Domestic money transfers within the United States
In-person money pickup at agent locations
Convenient location access across multiple states
Both web and mobile transaction options
